slices.name: Storage Foundation
slices.thesis: Map the persistence layer: SQLite store initialization, migrations, and the repository pattern (channels, messages, message_summaries, syncstate) that every other package depends on.
hints: storage.go defines core types (Channel, Message, MessageSummary, SyncState) and the sqliteStore struct — start here
channels.go, messages.go, message_summaries.go, syncstate.go are repository implementations that embed/reference sqliteStore
migrations.go is called from storage.go during initialization
This is the most-depended-upon package (7 packages, 62+ inbound calls) — understanding its interface is prerequisite for all other slices

slices.name: Telegram Client
slices.thesis: Map the low-level Telegram API client: authentication flow, session persistence, message fetching, and how it wraps the underlying TDLib/MTProto layer.
hints: client.go has 4 functions — likely the Client struct with connect/disconnect/fetch methods
auth.go has TerminalAuth (7 functions) — interactive authentication flow
session.go has FileSessionStorage (2 functions) — session persistence
messages.go has Message type (3 functions) — message data model separate from storage.Message
Called heavily from cmd (12 calls) and from refresh (1 call via telegram.go adapter)

slices.name: Source System
slices.thesis: Map the content source abstraction: the Source interface, Registry pattern, and the four implementations (Telegram, HackerNews, Reddit, RSS) that normalize external content into SourceMessages.
hints: source.go defines SourceMessage type — the common data model for all sources
registry.go has Registry struct (6 functions) — source registration and lookup
Four implementations in sub-packages: hackernews/hn.go (HNSource), reddit/reddit.go (RedditSource), rss/rss.go (RSSSource), telegram/telegram.go (TelegramSource)
source/telegram depends on refresh/telegram.go (fetcher interface) — watch for this bridge
hackernews and rss directly call storage types — may bypass the Source abstraction for sync state

slices.name: Summarizer
slices.thesis: Map the LLM summarization pipeline: the Summarizer interface, Service orchestrator, prompt construction, and the two LLM backends (Ollama local, OpenRouter cloud).
hints: summarizer.go defines interfaces and types (ChatRequest, ChatMessage, MessageSummarizeResult, SummarizeQueueResult) — the contract
service.go has Service struct (5 functions) — orchestrates fetching unsummarized messages, calling LLM, storing results
prompt.go (1 function) — likely builds the LLM prompt from messages
ollama.go (OllamaClient, 6 functions) and openrouter.go (OpenRouterClient, 2 functions) — two backend implementations
Service calls storage (14 calls) and source/registry (2 calls) — reads messages and writes summaries

slices.name: Refresh Pipeline
slices.thesis: Map the message refresh orchestration: how Service coordinates fetching new messages from sources and persisting them to storage, including the TelegramFetcher adapter.
hints: refresh.go has Service struct (5 functions), RefreshResult, FetchedMessage — the core orchestrator
telegram.go has TelegramFetcher (3 functions) — adapts internal/telegram.Client for the refresh pipeline
Heavy dependency on storage (17 cross-pkg calls) — manages channels, messages, and sync state
Small slice but high coupling — bridges telegram client and source system into storage

slices.name: TUI
slices.thesis: Map the terminal UI layer: the Bubbletea App model, view composition (channels, dashboard, filter, help), message types, key bindings, and how it triggers refresh/summarize operations.
hints: app.go (App struct, 18 functions) is the central Bubbletea model — handles Init/Update/View plus commands
types.go defines all message types (15 types) — the Bubbletea message vocabulary
channels.go (channelsModel, 7 functions) and dashboard.go (dashboardModel, 6 functions) — two main views
filter.go (SourceFilter) and filter_overlay.go (filterOverlayModel) — filtering subsystem
keys.go (keyMap) and styles.go — configuration/presentation
Bidirectional coupling with cmd (tui->cmd: 4, cmd->tui: 3) — likely through adapter interfaces defined in main.go
help.go (helpModel, 3 functions) — help overlay view

slices.name: CLI Orchestrator
slices.thesis: Map the application entry point: how main.go wires together config, storage, telegram, sources, refresh, summarizer, and TUI — including the adapter types that bridge packages.
hints: main.go is the highest fan-out file (10 packages) — classic orchestrator/wiring
Defines summarizeServiceAdapter and registryRefreshAdapter — bridges between packages
10 functions including main, buildLLMClient, ensureSourceChannel, storeChannel, storeMessages, updateSyncState
config.go (Config + 6 sub-config types, 3 functions) — loaded at startup, used to configure all services
Run this slice LAST — by then all boundary packages are mapped
